hawkish_phrases: some members argued that this instrument should only be used in contingency situations
Purchases of sovereign bonds were associated with a number of challenges and side effects
possible moral hazard implications for euro area governments could weaken their incentives for structural reforms
this should be addressed by macro-prudential policy
concerns about moral hazard it was argued that a regime of partial loss sharing would be more commensurate
dovish_phrases: the risk of second-round effects had increased further and, with it, the risk of too prolonged a period of too low inflation
monthly purchases of €60 billion which were intended to last until the end of September 2016
inflation dynamics had continued to be weaker than expected, economic slack had remained sizeable
Governing Council was thus faced with heightened risks of too prolonged a period of too low inflation
forceful policy action in addressing too low a level of inflation
summary: The Governing Council decided by large majority to launch an expanded asset purchase programme (QE) including sovereign bonds with monthly purchases of €60 billion from March 2015 to September 2016, with partial risk-sharing (20%), alongside TLTRO modifications, in response to inflation falling to -0.2% and heightened risks of too prolonged low inflation, despite some members expressing concerns about moral hazard and side effects.
